content.full_text:
OFFICE OF THE
CITY ATTORNEY

196 E. Fifth Street
P.O. Box 3420
Chico, CA 95927
(916) 895-4826

April 7, 1992

Deanne Stone, Esq.
Staff Counsel
Fair Political Practices
Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95812

Re:  Opinion on Disclosure Requirements for Bond Counsel

Dear Deanne:

To assist you in finding your way through the labyrinth of assessment district procedures which involve the retention of bond counsel, enclosed please find a copy of a portion of the California Debt Issuance Primer, published by the California Debt Advisory Commission, which contains a relatively brief description of assessment district procedures.

If I can assist you further in this regard, please don't hesitate to give me a call.

Yours very truly,

ROBERT G. BOEHM
Chico City Attorney

RGB/by

Enclosure

File: CA/20:34-8/Chrono

OFFICE OF THE
CITY ATTORNEY

196 E. Fifth Street
P.O. Box 3420
Chico, CA 95927
(916) 895-4826

January 16, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Chief Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Disclosure Statement Requirements for Bond Counsel

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

The City of Chico, like many other public agencies in the State of
California, frequently hires special legal counsel to provide legal
services in connection with the issuance of municipal tax-exempt
bonds including, but not limited to, general obligations bonds,
assessment district bonds, revenue bonds and Mello-Roos bonds. The
legal services they provide to the agency issuing the bonds consist of
legal advice concerning compliance with the procedural statute or
ordinance under which the bonds are issued and an opinion,
generally relied upon by the municipal bond market, that the bonds
being issued by the public agency are, in fact, tax-exempt.

Like most city attorneys and other public agency legal counsel, I
feel that I have the expertise to independently review advice given
by bond counsel in regard to compliance with the particular
procedural statute or ordinance under which the bonds are issued.
However, very few city attorneys or other public agency counsel
have the expertise to review opinions on the taxability of a bond
issue.

When retaining bond counsel, the question always comes up as to
whether they should be filing disclosure statements. Bond counsel
usually take the position that they are providing legal services
specific to only one particular project, i.e., the particular bond
issue, and that the ultimate decision as to whether to issue the
bonds is always vested in the legislative bodies of the public
agencies who retain them. Accordingly, they assert that they
should not be obliged to file disclosure statements under current
Fair Political Practices Commission regulations.

Page 1 of 2

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.                                    January 16, 1992


The contrary position is that when bound counsel are retained in
connection with a particular bond issue, they bring to the project
a special expertise on tax matters which generally is not and
cannot be reviewed by the local agency. Indeed, the essence of
their service is the rendition of an expert tax opinion that will
be accepted by the bond market and make the bonds saleable.

It would be most appreciated if the Fair Political Practices
Commission could render a formal opinion or regulation on the
applicability of the disclosure requirements to bond counsel being
retained in connection with the issuance of tax-exempt municipal
bonds.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. If I can provide you
with further information to assist you in rendering the requested
opinion, I would be happy to do so.

Yours very truly,


ROBERT G. BOEHM
Chico City Attorney

RGB/by

cc: City Manager Davis

File: CA/REF/10:90/Chrono




                                        Page 2 of 2
